changes.updateNote: New information about Typhoon Bavi ('Warnings and insurance' page).

sections.heading: Typhoon Bavi
added: Typhoon Bavi is expected to have its strongest impact on Taiwan from 10 July to 11 July.
The Central Weather Administration is forecasting heavy to torrential rain, strong winds, rough seas and possible transport disruptions.
The greatest rainfall risk is for central and northern Taiwan.
Flash flooding and landslides are possible, particularly in mountainous areas, while coastal areas may experience large waves.
Monitor official weather warnings, check transport schedules, and follow the advice of local authorities.

changes.description: Inhoud van het advies gewijzigd (zonder nieuwe brondatum).

sections.heading: Typhoon Bavi
added: Typhoon Bavi is expected to pass northeast of Taiwan, affecting the northern part of the island, including Taipei, on July 10.
The storm is likely to bring excessive rainfall and violent winds.

It may cause flash flooding and landslides and could severely disrupt the following essential services: transportation power distribution water and food supply telecommunications networks emergency services medical care If you are in the affected area: exercis

changes.updateNote: Information about wildfires ('Warnings and insurance' page).

sections.heading: Wildfires
added: Wildfires can occur across France during the summer months.
The risk is highest in the south of the country, particularly along the Mediterranean coast, in Corsica, and in areas of the south-west during periods of hot, dry weather.
Check the French weather service’s fire alert map before travelling and monitor local authority advice during your stay.
Conditions can change quickly and access to forests, countryside areas and tourist sites may be restricted at short notice.

changes.description: Inhoud van het advies gewijzigd (zonder nieuwe brondatum).

sections.heading: Vigipirate plan
added: The current level is at 2, “vigilance renforcée” (“enhanced vigiliance”).

sections.heading: Pre-travel vaccines and medications
added: About Yellow Fever Yellow Fever Vaccination Centres in Canada Hepatitis B Hepatitis B is a risk in every destination.
Learn more: Rabies: Symptoms and treatment Tick-borne encephalitisTick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is a risk in some areas of this destination.
Measles Measles is a serious viral infection that spreads easily.

sections.heading: Tick and insect bite prevention
added: To protect yourself from bites: use an approved bug spray (insect repellent) on exposed skin wear light-coloured, loose clothing made of tightly woven materials like nylon or polyester wear socks and closed-toe shoes sleep under mosquito netting if you’re outd
Dengue In this country, risk of dengue is sporadic.

sections.heading: Forest and maquis fires
added: Forest and maquis fires often occur in summer, particularly in the south of the country and in Corsica.
In recent years, due to high heat and drought conditions, the forest fire season has lengthened and broadened to cover the entire country.
The risk of major fires has also increased.

sections.heading: Safe food and water precautions
added: Take precautions by following these tips: Boil it, cook it, peel it, or leave it (only eat food that is prepared safely) Avoid, if possible, getting water in your eyes, mouth, or nose when swimming in freshwater (like streams, lakes, or canals), especially aft
Travellers' diarrheaTravellers' diarrhea is the most common illness that affects travellers.
Learn more: Typhoid fever Eating and drinking safely abroad Clean your hands to help reduce the spread of infectious diseases

sections.heading: Pre-travel vaccines and medications
added: Japanese encephalitisJapanese encephalitis is a viral infection that can cause swelling of the brain.
Learn more: Rabies: Symptoms and treatment Yellow Fever - Country Entry Requirements Yellow fever is a disease caused by a flavivirus from the bite of an infected mosquito.
Learn more: Measles Polio – WHO Temporary Recommendations Polio (poliomyelitis) is an infectious disease that can be prevented by vaccination.

changes.updateNote: New information about recent cases of drug-assisted robbery and sexual assault (see ‘Drug assisted robbery and sexual assault’ on the ‘Safety and security’ page).

sections.heading: Drug assisted robbery and sexual assault
added: There have been reports of criminals in Trinidad and Tobago using drugs, deception and sexual solicitation, including via dating apps, to facilitate robbery and sexual assault.
Victims may be lured and drugged or beaten and robbed.
Incidents have occurred in bars, nightclubs, hotels and on city streets, sometimes involving groups working together.

You should: avoid accepting invitations from strangers or inviting them to your accommodation take care when using dating apps keep food and drinks under supervision plan travel routes in advance, particularly at night If drugged, seek local medical attention

added: Hepatitis B Hepatitis B is a risk in every destination.

sections.heading: Safe food and water precautions
added: Take precautions by following these tips: Boil it, cook it, peel it, or leave it (only eat food that is prepared safely) Avoid, if possible, getting water in your eyes, mouth, or nose when swimming in freshwater (like streams, lakes, or canals), especially aft
CholeraRisk Cholera is a risk in parts of this country.
